result: {"verdict":"findings","guard_confirmed":true,"reviewed_sha":"0e4872162f09120188404d5d23448ff3a6298133","base_sha":"e41b87173f2073a9b6694a62813eddc14a7844ad","findings":[{"title":"fetch_windows_file decodes raw SSH stdout with base64 validate=True, rejecting any channel noise","evidence":"scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:518-520 pipes entire wssh stdout into python3 b64decode(..., validate=True). Empirically verified: validate=True rejects trailing \\r\\n, \\n, and banner-prefixed payloads (binascii.Error 'Non-base64 digit found'). The identical wire pattern at line 721 uses the lenient base64.decode (verified to tolerate \\r\\n), and every other wssh consumer sanitizes with tr -d '\\r' | tail -1 (e.g. windows_sha256 line 100, manifest tail 504, topology 896). wssh does not pass -NoProfile, so PowerShell profile/banner output reaches stdout.","predicted_failure":"On the first live run against a Windows host whose SSH/PowerShell session emits a profile line, banner, or trailing CR, every Windows-to-q fetch fails: fixture manifests, all collect_windows_component evidence, and the durable runtime-swap record (prepare_windows_runtime ~line 995). The harness dies/voids before any arm completes - a total fail-closed non-start on a path the findings doc confirms was never executed live.","severity":"MEDIUM","better_approach":"Sanitize before decoding at both sites the same way the rest of the script does (strip whitespace/CR and take the final line, or emit a sentinel-delimited payload), or use the lenient decoder consistently and rely on the existing local_hash == remote_hash check at line 522 for integrity."},{"title":"Hard crash followed by rerun records the leftover test daemon as the preserved baseline","evidence":"scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:935-980: WIN_PRIOR_DAEMON (retained-before-$SESSION_TAG) and the runtime-swap-intent record are both session-tag-scoped; prepare_windows_runtime sets $hadPrior/$priorHash from whatever currently sits at $WIN_ACTIVE_DAEMON with no comparison against WIN_STAGED_DAEMON_SHA and no detection of leftover retained-before-* entries or intent records from a previous crashed session. Restoration runs only via on_exit, which a power-loss-class crash skips; a rerun mints a new SESSION_TAG and never consults the old record.","predicted_failure":"Crash mid-session leaves the test daemon active. The rerun hashes it as prior_sha, swaps, and on completion faithfully 'restores' the test daemon as the machine baseline. The true original daemon is stranded at retained-before-<old-tag>-blit-daemon.exe and never reinstated, silently violating the documented invariant that any pre-existing Windows daemon is restored exactly. Non-destructive (bytes remain on disk) but undetected and not auto-recovered.","severity":"MEDIUM","better_approach":"On startup, refuse to proceed (or require explicit operator acknowledgement) when the active daemon hash equals the staged test daemon SHA or when any retained-before-* entry or prior swap-intent record exists under WIN_SESSION_ROOT; alternatively store the swap intent at a fixed session-independent path and reconcile it before classifying the baseline."},{"title":"Orphan-client fallback reap silently skips PIDs below 10000 due to global IFS vs ps column padding","evidence":"scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:11 sets IFS=$'\\n\\t' (no space); stop_q_client lines 1360-1361 iterate $(ps -axo pid=) and require [[ $candidate =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]. Empirically verified on this host: unquoted expansion keeps the 5-wide right-justified padding, so '    1' and '  265' fail the regex and are skipped while '12345' matches - the fallback can only ever find PIDs >= 10000.","predicted_failure":"In the recovery path where CURRENT_Q_CLIENT_PID is empty and the fsync'd client.pid file is missing, a still-running orphan blit client with a sub-10000 PID is never found; stop_q_client returns clean, bypassing run_arm's 'client survived owned execution' void (line 2113), and the arm proceeds while a stale client may still be writing the destination. Trigger is narrow (pid file written and fsync'd at launch, lines 1691-1694) and downstream manifest comparison would usually catch corruption, but the safety net is structurally defeated and fails silently.","severity":"MEDIUM","better_approach":"Strip the padding before matching, e.g. iterate $(ps -axo pid= | tr -d ' ') or use awk '{print $1}'; alternatively localize IFS=$' \\n\\t' for the loop. One-line fix restoring the guard for all PIDs."},{"title":"REVIEW_REQUIRED and clean analyses are indistinguishable to automation: both exit 0 and the harness gates only on exit status","evidence":"scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py:2531-2535 sets status=REVIEW_REQUIRED when arm_review_count, decision_review_count, or performance_review_count is non-zero, but main() (2725-2742) returns 0 for both statuses and 2 only for refusals. scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:2478-2487 runs the analyzer with '|| session_void', so on exit 0 it writes analyzer-result.txt, writes the completion marker, sets SESSION_COMPLETE=1, clears traps, and retains the session identically for both outcomes. Verified by direct read of both call sites.","predicted_failure":"A 96-arm run that detects the exact effect the experiment exists to find - paired role-decision skew or >1.10 initiator-layout performance skew - completes with exit 0, MEASUREMENTS-COMPLETE, and a full analysis tree indistinguishable at the exit-code/marker level from a clean run. Any consumer or follow-up tooling keying on exit status or marker presence silently treats the skewed result as passing; the signal survives only if a human reads the status text inside analyzer-result.txt.","severity":"MEDIUM","better_approach":"Give REVIEW_REQUIRED a distinct exit code (e.g. 3) with the harness handling 0/3 explicitly, or have the harness parse the status token and write a distinct REVIEW-REQUIRED marker file so downstream automation cannot conflate the two outcomes."},{"title":"Analyzer over-constrains accepted settlements to live_streams == target, contradicting dial.rs's documented partial-settle contract","evidence":"scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py:1627 raises 'accepted settlement reason/live count mismatch' unless event live_streams == target; control-lane checks at 1937-1945 likewise require resize_ack_received.live_streams == target and source_settled.live_streams == target with accepted=True. crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs resize_settled (doc at ~709-716, verified) explicitly defines effective_streams as 'the live count now in effect (from the peer's ack, or the local count if a post-ack dial failed and nothing changed)' - i.e. production legitimately settles an accepted epoch with effective != target.","predicted_failure":"A real transfer whose peer acks an ADD but whose post-ack local socket dial fails (live stays 4, target 5, accepted=true) emits a settlement the analyzer refuses, exiting 2 and voiding the entire 96-arm session via session_void - hours of measurement discarded over a legitimate, documented runtime behavior. This path has never run end-to-end, so the first live occurrence is the first test of it.","severity":"MEDIUM","better_approach":"Accept the documented partial-settle case: for accepted settlements require live_streams to equal the effective count carried by the settlement (cross-checked between data-plane and control-lane events) rather than forcing it to equal target, flagging partial settles for review instead of refusing the session."},{"title":"cooldown/sustain/bound dial-acceptance branches have zero test coverage; suite is synthetic-only","evidence":"grep verified: '\"cooldown\"', '\"sustain\"', '\"bound\"' each occur 0 times in scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze_test.py (1821 lines, 72 tests); all tests drive a hand-authored SyntheticSession rather than real harness/runtime output. Consistent with my mutation guard: disabling the arm-verdict downgrade was detected by exactly one failing test. Delegated differential fuzz of DialPolicyReplay against an independent transcription of dial.rs found zero divergences, so current behavior is correct but unguarded.","predicted_failure":"A future regression in the cooldown/sustain/bound acceptance logic of DialPolicyReplay/_validate_dial (analyzer ~1372-1391, 1538-1590) passes all 72 tests, and the analyzer then refuses valid live evidence (voiding sessions) or accepts invalid decision sequences, discovered only during an expensive live 96-arm run.","severity":"LOW","better_approach":"Add synthetic trace tests exercising all ten decision reasons (cooldown, sustain, bound, hysteresis-accept included) through _validate_dial, and a fixture generated by the real emitter once available, so the runtime-trace/analyzer contract is bound by tests rather than first live use."},{"title":"Evidence-integrity gaps: duplicate JSON keys collapse silently and blocked_ratio cross-check tolerance is far wider than 1 ULP","evidence":"scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py loads trace lines with json.loads and no object_pairs_hook (grep: 0 occurrences); verified {\"reason\":\"idle\",\"reason\":\"add\"} parses to {'reason': 'add'}, and _event_exact's set()-based field comparison cannot see the duplication. Separately, line 1512 cross-checks the emitted blocked_ratio against raw counters with math.isclose(abs_tol=1e-12) while the replay then uses the emitted value verbatim.","predicted_failure":"A hand-edited or corrupted evidence line carrying duplicated decision fields (two reason/blocked_ratio/live_streams keys) is accepted last-wins and passes every exactness/integrity check; a forged blocked_ratio within 1e-12 of the counter-derived value but on the opposite side of the 0.05/0.30 thresholds replays as the forger intends. Unreachable via the serde producer, so this weakens only the tamper-evidence goal of the immutable evidence tree, not live correctness.","severity":"LOW","better_approach":"Load trace JSON with an object_pairs_hook that rejects duplicate keys, and tighten the blocked_ratio cross-check to exact equality of the shortest round-trip representation (or a <=1 ULP comparison), matching the fail-closed posture used everywhere else."}]}

findings.title: fetch_windows_file decodes raw SSH stdout with base64 validate=True, rejecting any channel noise
findings.evidence:
scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:518-520 pipes entire wssh stdout into python3 b64decode(..., validate=True). Empirically verified: validate=True rejects trailing 
, 
, and banner-prefixed payloads (binascii.Error 'Non-base64 digit found'). The identical wire pattern at line 721 uses the lenient base64.decode (verified to tolerate 
), and every other wssh consumer sanitizes with tr -d '' | tail -1 (e.g. windows_sha256 line 100, manifest tail 504, topology 896). wssh does not pass -NoProfile, so PowerShell profile/banner output reaches stdout.
findings.predicted_failure: On the first live run against a Windows host whose SSH/PowerShell session emits a profile line, banner, or trailing CR, every Windows-to-q fetch fails: fixture manifests, all collect_windows_component evidence, and the durable runtime-swap record (prepare_windows_runtime ~line 995). The harness dies/voids before any arm completes - a total fail-closed non-start on a path the findings doc confirms was never executed live.
findings.better_approach: Sanitize before decoding at both sites the same way the rest of the script does (strip whitespace/CR and take the final line, or emit a sentinel-delimited payload), or use the lenient decoder consistently and rely on the existing local_hash == remote_hash check at line 522 for integrity.

findings.title: Hard crash followed by rerun records the leftover test daemon as the preserved baseline
findings.evidence: scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:935-980: WIN_PRIOR_DAEMON (retained-before-$SESSION_TAG) and the runtime-swap-intent record are both session-tag-scoped; prepare_windows_runtime sets $hadPrior/$priorHash from whatever currently sits at $WIN_ACTIVE_DAEMON with no comparison against WIN_STAGED_DAEMON_SHA and no detection of leftover retained-before-* entries or intent records from a previous crashed session. Restoration runs only via on_exit, which a power-loss-class crash skips; a rerun mints a new SESSION_TAG and never consults the old record.
findings.predicted_failure: Crash mid-session leaves the test daemon active. The rerun hashes it as prior_sha, swaps, and on completion faithfully 'restores' the test daemon as the machine baseline. The true original daemon is stranded at retained-before-<old-tag>-blit-daemon.exe and never reinstated, silently violating the documented invariant that any pre-existing Windows daemon is restored exactly. Non-destructive (bytes remain on disk) but undetected and not auto-recovered.
findings.better_approach: On startup, refuse to proceed (or require explicit operator acknowledgement) when the active daemon hash equals the staged test daemon SHA or when any retained-before-* entry or prior swap-intent record exists under WIN_SESSION_ROOT; alternatively store the swap intent at a fixed session-independent path and reconcile it before classifying the baseline.

findings.title: Orphan-client fallback reap silently skips PIDs below 10000 due to global IFS vs ps column padding
findings.evidence: scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:11 sets IFS=$'\n\t' (no space); stop_q_client lines 1360-1361 iterate $(ps -axo pid=) and require [[ $candidate =~ ^[0-9]+$ ]]. Empirically verified on this host: unquoted expansion keeps the 5-wide right-justified padding, so '    1' and '  265' fail the regex and are skipped while '12345' matches - the fallback can only ever find PIDs >= 10000.
findings.predicted_failure: In the recovery path where CURRENT_Q_CLIENT_PID is empty and the fsync'd client.pid file is missing, a still-running orphan blit client with a sub-10000 PID is never found; stop_q_client returns clean, bypassing run_arm's 'client survived owned execution' void (line 2113), and the arm proceeds while a stale client may still be writing the destination. Trigger is narrow (pid file written and fsync'd at launch, lines 1691-1694) and downstream manifest comparison would usually catch corruption, but the safety net is structurally defeated and fails silently.
findings.better_approach: Strip the padding before matching, e.g. iterate $(ps -axo pid= | tr -d ' ') or use awk '{print $1}'; alternatively localize IFS=$' \n\t' for the loop. One-line fix restoring the guard for all PIDs.

findings.title: REVIEW_REQUIRED and clean analyses are indistinguishable to automation: both exit 0 and the harness gates only on exit status
findings.evidence: scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py:2531-2535 sets status=REVIEW_REQUIRED when arm_review_count, decision_review_count, or performance_review_count is non-zero, but main() (2725-2742) returns 0 for both statuses and 2 only for refusals. scripts/bench_ldt4_rigw.sh:2478-2487 runs the analyzer with '|| session_void', so on exit 0 it writes analyzer-result.txt, writes the completion marker, sets SESSION_COMPLETE=1, clears traps, and retains the session identically for both outcomes. Verified by direct read of both call sites.
findings.predicted_failure: A 96-arm run that detects the exact effect the experiment exists to find - paired role-decision skew or >1.10 initiator-layout performance skew - completes with exit 0, MEASUREMENTS-COMPLETE, and a full analysis tree indistinguishable at the exit-code/marker level from a clean run. Any consumer or follow-up tooling keying on exit status or marker presence silently treats the skewed result as passing; the signal survives only if a human reads the status text inside analyzer-result.txt.
findings.better_approach: Give REVIEW_REQUIRED a distinct exit code (e.g. 3) with the harness handling 0/3 explicitly, or have the harness parse the status token and write a distinct REVIEW-REQUIRED marker file so downstream automation cannot conflate the two outcomes.

findings.title: Analyzer over-constrains accepted settlements to live_streams == target, contradicting dial.rs's documented partial-settle contract
findings.evidence: scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py:1627 raises 'accepted settlement reason/live count mismatch' unless event live_streams == target; control-lane checks at 1937-1945 likewise require resize_ack_received.live_streams == target and source_settled.live_streams == target with accepted=True. crates/blit-core/src/dial.rs resize_settled (doc at ~709-716, verified) explicitly defines effective_streams as 'the live count now in effect (from the peer's ack, or the local count if a post-ack dial failed and nothing changed)' - i.e. production legitimately settles an accepted epoch with effective != target.
findings.predicted_failure: A real transfer whose peer acks an ADD but whose post-ack local socket dial fails (live stays 4, target 5, accepted=true) emits a settlement the analyzer refuses, exiting 2 and voiding the entire 96-arm session via session_void - hours of measurement discarded over a legitimate, documented runtime behavior. This path has never run end-to-end, so the first live occurrence is the first test of it.
findings.better_approach: Accept the documented partial-settle case: for accepted settlements require live_streams to equal the effective count carried by the settlement (cross-checked between data-plane and control-lane events) rather than forcing it to equal target, flagging partial settles for review instead of refusing the session.

findings.title: cooldown/sustain/bound dial-acceptance branches have zero test coverage; suite is synthetic-only
findings.evidence: grep verified: '"cooldown"', '"sustain"', '"bound"' each occur 0 times in scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze_test.py (1821 lines, 72 tests); all tests drive a hand-authored SyntheticSession rather than real harness/runtime output. Consistent with my mutation guard: disabling the arm-verdict downgrade was detected by exactly one failing test. Delegated differential fuzz of DialPolicyReplay against an independent transcription of dial.rs found zero divergences, so current behavior is correct but unguarded.
findings.predicted_failure: A future regression in the cooldown/sustain/bound acceptance logic of DialPolicyReplay/_validate_dial (analyzer ~1372-1391, 1538-1590) passes all 72 tests, and the analyzer then refuses valid live evidence (voiding sessions) or accepts invalid decision sequences, discovered only during an expensive live 96-arm run.
findings.better_approach: Add synthetic trace tests exercising all ten decision reasons (cooldown, sustain, bound, hysteresis-accept included) through _validate_dial, and a fixture generated by the real emitter once available, so the runtime-trace/analyzer contract is bound by tests rather than first live use.

findings.title: Evidence-integrity gaps: duplicate JSON keys collapse silently and blocked_ratio cross-check tolerance is far wider than 1 ULP
findings.evidence: scripts/ldt4_rigw_analyze.py loads trace lines with json.loads and no object_pairs_hook (grep: 0 occurrences); verified {"reason":"idle","reason":"add"} parses to {'reason': 'add'}, and _event_exact's set()-based field comparison cannot see the duplication. Separately, line 1512 cross-checks the emitted blocked_ratio against raw counters with math.isclose(abs_tol=1e-12) while the replay then uses the emitted value verbatim.
findings.predicted_failure: A hand-edited or corrupted evidence line carrying duplicated decision fields (two reason/blocked_ratio/live_streams keys) is accepted last-wins and passes every exactness/integrity check; a forged blocked_ratio within 1e-12 of the counter-derived value but on the opposite side of the 0.05/0.30 thresholds replays as the forger intends. Unreachable via the serde producer, so this weakens only the tamper-evidence goal of the immutable evidence tree, not live correctness.
findings.better_approach: Load trace JSON with an object_pairs_hook that rejects duplicate keys, and tighten the blocked_ratio cross-check to exact equality of the shortest round-trip representation (or a <=1 ULP comparison), matching the fail-closed posture used everywhere else.
